title: Flavorful Argentinian Bonito Flatbread
description: A wine-infused, chewy gluten‑free flatbread topped with grilled bonito and a smoky glaze, evoking nostalgic Argentinian flavors.
instructions: Preheat a cast iron skillet over medium heat and lightly brush it with olive oil.
Season the bonito fillet with salt, pepper, smoked paprika, cumin, and a tablespoon of olive oil, then set aside.
Sauté the minced garlic and thinly sliced onion in the skillet until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the red wine to the skillet, reduce it by half, and stir in lemon juice and dried oregano until the liquid thickens into a glaze.
Place the seasoned bonito fillet into the skillet, cook each side for 3 minutes, then remove and slice into thin strips.
Warm the gluten-free naan in the skillet for 1 minute per side until it becomes chewy and slightly charred.
Spread the wine glaze over the warm naan, top with the sliced bonito, sprinkle chopped parsley, and drizzle with remaining olive oil.
Finish with a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds and serve immediately.
